zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford於1145年出生在英國諾圖波藍德阿恩維克時，她的父親Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria31歲，母親Ada de Warenne25歲。她於大約1160年與Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1201年在英國越可爾里士滿享年56歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾薩福崔 聖 朱迪斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford于1145年出生在英国諾圖波藍德阿恩維克时，她的父亲Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria31岁，母亲Ada de Warenne25岁。她于大约1160年与Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1201年在英国越可爾里士滿享年56岁，埋葬在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾薩福崔 聖 朱迪斯。

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Hereford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Hertford (Hertfordshire) (in which the t was lost early, as in the modern pronunciation Harford), Hereford (Herefordshire), or Harford (Devon, Gloucestershire). The Devon and Herefordshire placenames derive from Old English here ‘army’ + ford ‘ford’, while the Gloucestershire and Hertfordshire placenames derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + ford.

variants.name: Herford
meanings: English: variant of   Hereford  .
German: variant of   Herfurth  .
German: habitational name from a place of that name in Westphalia.

variants.name: Heryford
meanings: Altered form of English   Hereford  .

variants.name: Heriford
meanings: Altered form of English   Hereford  .

variants.name: Harford
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): variant of   Hereford  .

variants.name: Havard
meanings: English and Welsh (Brecknockshire and Glamorgan): habitational name from Haverfordwest (Pembrokeshire), from Old English hæfer ‘goat’ + ford ‘ford’.
English and Welsh (Brecknockshire and Glamorgan): in some cases, the name may also be a variant of   Hereford  .
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hahward, composed of the elements hah, which is unexplained, and ward ‘guard’.

variants.name: Hurford
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): variant of   Hereford  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Huntington  .

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford was born in 1145, in Alnwick, Northumberland, England, her father, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, was 31 and her mother, Ada de Warenne, was 25. She married Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne about 1160. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1201, in Richmond, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 56, and was buried in Sawtry St Judith, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford est née en 1145, à Alnwick, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Ada de Warenne, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne environ 1160. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1201, à Richmond, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Sawtry St Judith, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford 1145 in Alnwick, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria 31 und ihre Mutter Ada de Warenne 25. Sie hat um 1160 Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1201 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Richmond, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Sawtry St Judith, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne circa 1160. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1201, nel Richmond, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolta a Sawtry St Judith, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Herefordが1145年にイギリスノースウンバーランドアルンウィックで生まれた時，父Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbriaは31歳，母Ada de Warenneは25歳であった。約1160年にConan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1201年にイギリスヨークシュアイアリチモンドで56歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアサウトリー スト ジュディスにて埋葬された。

ko.Sawtry St. Judith, Huntingdonshire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 사w트리 스트 주디스
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford(이)가 1145년 영국 노르스벌랜드 알윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ada de Warenne(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1160년 Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1201년 영국 요르크실르 리ch몽에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 사w트리 스트 주디스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford родилась в 1145 г. в местности алнвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Ada de Warenne, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne около 1160 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1201 г. в местности ричмонд, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности савтри ст джудит, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Sawtry St. Judith, Huntingdonshire, Engl: Sawtry St Judith, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret of Huntingdon Countess of Hereford nació en 1145, en Alnwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Ada de Warenne, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Conan IV de PENTHIEVRE Duc de Bretagne aproximadamente 1160. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1201, en Richmond, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Sawtry St Judith, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
